title: DuckingEffect
description: Sidechain compressor (ducker) implemented as an WorkletEffect. Attenuates the main audio signal whenever the `sidechain` AudioBus exceeds the threshold, then releases when it drops back down. Commonly used to duck background music under dialogue or sound effects. Attack and release times are expressed in milliseconds and converted internally to one-pole filter coefficients.

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: Sidechain compressor (ducker) implemented as an WorkletEffect. Attenuates the main audio signal whenever the `sidechain` AudioBus exceeds the threshold, then releases when it drops back down. Commonly used to duck background music under dialogue or sound effects. Attack and release times are expressed in milliseconds and converted internally to one-pole filter coefficients.

members.name: _onWorkletReady
members.description: Subclass hook — called once the worklet is loaded and inserted into the wet path. Use for additional wiring (e.g. a sidechain input).

members.name: destroy
members.description: Disconnects all nodes, cancels any pending worklet load, and releases resources.

members.name: attackMs
members.description: Attack time in milliseconds — how quickly the ducker engages. Default 30.

members.name: inputNode
members.description: The node where audio enters this effect.

members.name: outputNode
members.description: The node where audio exits this effect.

members.name: ratio
members.description: Compression ratio applied to the main signal above the threshold. Range 1..20, default 4.

members.name: ready
members.description: Resolves once the worklet is loaded and inserted into the wet path.

members.name: releaseMs
members.description: Release time in milliseconds — how quickly the ducker disengages. Default 300.

members.name: threshold
members.description: Sidechain level above which the main signal is attenuated, in dBFS. Range −100..0, default −20.

members.name: wet
members.description: Wet (processed) mix level, 0..1. The dry level is automatically 1 - wet. Ramped smoothly.
